postData.contentHtml:
Jacksonville, FL – In a heartwarming display of community spirit and international solidarity, Action News Jax and Telemundo Jacksonville have launched a joint earthquake relief drive for Venezuela. This initiative comes in response to the catastrophic earthquakes that have recently rocked the South American nation, causing widespread devastation and a significant loss of life.

The powerful tremors have inflicted immense suffering, with Venezuela's Health Minister reporting approximately 235 fatalities and over 4,300 injuries. Thousands more are reported missing, and critical infrastructure, including the country's main airport, has sustained damage, complicating relief efforts.

In the wake of this disaster, the United States has pledged a substantial and rapid response. Secretary of State Marco Rubio affirmed a "whole-of-government response" that promises to be "big, fast, and effective." This commitment includes the allocation of $150 million to various aid groups and UN agencies, alongside the deployment of Disaster Assistant Response Teams and search and rescue teams from Virginia and California, complete with specialized K9 units.

Here in Jacksonville, the relief drive, hosted at the Telemundo Jacksonville studios, aims to gather much-needed supplies to assist the affected families. The community's response has been largely positive on social media, with many expressing support for the humanitarian effort, though some discussions and debates are also present.

This concerted effort by local media, combined with international aid, underscores a collective resolve to provide urgent assistance and support to Venezuela during this challenging time.
postData.title: Jacksonville Rises to Aid Venezuela After Catastrophic Earthquakes
postData.summary: Action News Jax and Telemundo Jacksonville are spearheading a relief drive to support Venezuelan families devastated by recent powerful earthquakes that have left hundreds dead and thousands injured.

sources.title: Today: Donate supplies to the Venezuela relief effort at Telemundo Jacksonville studios
Action News Jax hosts earthquake relief drive for Venezuela
Venezuela health minister says around 235 people dead and 4,300 injured in catastrophic earthquakes
U.S. pledges generous earthquake relief to Venezuela
